patient.pharmacy: Hannaford Pharmacy, 2985 Brighton Avenue, Portland, Maine 04102

condition.name: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
condition.note: Trial period of dietary change before any decision on insulin. Patient is engaged and motivated.

condition.name: Osteoarthritis
condition.note: Hands and lower back. Worse in the mornings.

condition.name: Mild cataracts, bilateral
condition.note: Right eye more advanced. Annual screening with Dr. Patterson.

condition.name: Elevated LDL cholesterol
condition.note: LDL 148 mg/dL. Statin declined in favor of dietary approaches.

medication.name: Metformin Hydrochloride
medication.note: Daily total 2000 mg. Take with food, never on an empty stomach.

dose.timing: Morning, 30 minutes before breakfast, about 7:00 AM

medication.name: Hydrochlorothiazide
medication.note: Diuretic. Drink adequate water through the day. Avoid taking late in the day.

support.name: Cinnamon tea
support.note: Tolerated by the physician, used alongside the Metformin, not in place of it.

support.name: Daily walk
support.detail: One mile with a neighbor at 7:00 AM, weather permitting

carePlan.allergies: No known drug allergies. No known food allergies documented.

followUp.item: Next A1c assessment recommended within twelve weeks of the January visit.
Continue daily fasting glucose log and home blood pressure readings.
Annual diabetic eye screening to continue with Dr. Patterson.
Dental cleaning every six months. Flu and COVID vaccines each October at the church clinic.
